text: The Athletic are reporting that Declan Rice will miss their game against Aston Villa this evening with a minor knee injury.
They also say they are “hopeful” that Rice’s absence “will be offset by the return of Jurrien Timber “.
Kai Havertz (knee) and Ben White (hamstring) are reportedly with the squad.
Kai Havertz (knee) and Ben White (hamstring) are reportedly with the squad.
Riccardo Calafiori remains a doubt, while Max Dowman (ankle) and Cristhian Mosquera (ankle) are out.
Riccardo Calafiori remains a doubt, while Max Dowman (ankle) and Cristhian Mosquera (ankle) are out.
Riccardo Calafiori remains a doubt, while Max Dowman (ankle) and Cristhian Mosquera (ankle) are out.

Dango Ouattara and Frank Onyeka are at the Africa Cup of Nations with their respective countries, while long-term absentees Josh Dasilva (knee), Fábio Carvalho (knee) and Antoni Milambo (knee) are still unavailable.

Dango Ouattara and Frank Onyeka are at the Africa Cup of Nations with their respective countries, while long-term absentees Josh Dasilva (knee), Fábio Carvalho (knee) and Antoni Milambo (knee) are still unavailable.

Meanwhile, Andrews discussed the fact that Igor Thiago has been playing through the pain barrier – which perhaps explains why the striker hasn’t featured in any midweek game this season. Next up: a match on Thursday…
Lucas Bergvall should be okay after he came off in the match against Crystal Palace last weekend.
Destiny Udogie (hamstring), Dominic Solanke (ankle), James Maddison (knee) and Dejan Kulusevski (knee) remain out, however. Udogie is back running but not in team training.
Destiny Udogie (hamstring), Dominic Solanke (ankle), James Maddison (knee) and Dejan Kulusevski (knee) remain out, however. Udogie is back running but not in team training.

Xavi Simons is still suspended for this game, too, while Pape Matar Sarr and Yves Bissouma are both at the Africa Cup of Nations.
Xavi Simons is still suspended for this game, too, while Pape Matar Sarr and Yves Bissouma are both at the Africa Cup of Nations.
Xavi Simons is still suspended for this game, too, while Pape Matar Sarr and Yves Bissouma are both at the Africa Cup of Nations.
Cristian Romero returns from a one-match ban, at least. He faces a further misconduct charge but that won’t be resolved till later in January, so he’s available for selection in midweek.
So too is Brennan Johnson , who is reportedly close to a move elsewhere.
Joe Rodon will be out for around three weeks with the ankle injury he sustained at Sunderland on Sunday.
Daniel James (hamstring) and Sean Longstaff (calf) are still unavailable but, Rodon aside, Daniel Farke has no new concerns.
Daniel James (hamstring) and Sean Longstaff (calf) are still unavailable but, Rodon aside, Daniel Farke has no new concerns.
No questions on team news, with the time instead devoted to the transfer window, the title race and Viktor Gyokeres ‘ seven-match run without a goal from open play.
We refer you to our Scout Notes for quotes on Jurrien Timber and Riccardo Calafiori , who both missed out in Gameweek 18. Calafiori had been named in the starting XI but pulled out in the warm-up.
We refer you to our Scout Notes for quotes on Jurrien Timber and Riccardo Calafiori , who both missed out in Gameweek 18. Calafiori had been named in the starting XI but pulled out in the warm-up.
Gabriel Magalhaes (knee) returned as a substitute in that match.
Kai Havertz (knee), meanwhile, is back in training. Mikel Arteta said last Tuesday that a return for the German would be “a matter of days, if not weeks”.
Ben White (hamstring), Max Dowman (ankle) and Cristhian Mosquera (ankle), we think, remain out.
Ben White (hamstring), Max Dowman (ankle) and Cristhian Mosquera (ankle), we think, remain out.
Ben White (hamstring), Max Dowman (ankle) and Cristhian Mosquera (ankle), we think, remain out.
Bruno Fernandes (hamstring) will not be involved against Wolverhampton Wanderers but the midfielder is already pushing for a return to training.
Kobbie Mainoo (calf), Matthijs de Ligt (back) and Harry Maguire (hamstring) are also set to miss out, while Mason Mount will be assessed after he “felt something” in the win over Newcastle United.
Kobbie Mainoo (calf), Matthijs de Ligt (back) and Harry Maguire (hamstring) are also set to miss out, while Mason Mount will be assessed after he “felt something” in the win over Newcastle United.

Bryan Mbeumo , Amad Diallo and Noussair Mazraoui are on international duty.
Bryan Mbeumo , Amad Diallo and Noussair Mazraoui are on international duty.
Bryan Mbeumo , Amad Diallo and Noussair Mazraoui are on international duty.
Firstly, he was asked if Sven Botman (back) or Kieran Trippier (hamstring) would be back.
Firstly, he was asked if Sven Botman (back) or Kieran Trippier (hamstring) would be back.
Dan Burn (rib) is out until January, while there wasn’t a timeline on Tino Livramento (knee), the last we heard.
Dan Burn (rib) is out until January, while there wasn’t a timeline on Tino Livramento (knee), the last we heard.
Second-stringers Emil Krafth (knee) and Will Osula (ankle) have been out since the November break, while Anthony Elanga missed out in Gameweek 18 with a knock.
Second-stringers Emil Krafth (knee) and Will Osula (ankle) have been out since the November break, while Anthony Elanga missed out in Gameweek 18 with a knock.
Second-stringers Emil Krafth (knee) and Will Osula (ankle) have been out since the November break, while Anthony Elanga missed out in Gameweek 18 with a knock.
Nick Pope (groin) was back in the squad on Friday, meanwhile. He was only a substitute, and it sounds like he would have been anyway regardless of a pre-match illness.
Yoane Wissa has been fit for a while and his manager was asked if he needed a run of starts now to show his best.
There’s nothing new on Marc Cucurella , then , after the left-back was taken off with hamstring discomfort in the defeat to Aston Villa.
As for the rest of the Blues’ team news, Dario Essugo (thigh), Romeo Lavia (quad) and Levi Colwill (knee) remain out. Mykhailo Mudryk is suspended, as he has been for the last 12 months.
As for the rest of the Blues’ team news, Dario Essugo (thigh), Romeo Lavia (quad) and Levi Colwill (knee) remain out. Mykhailo Mudryk is suspended, as he has been for the last 12 months.

As for the rest of the Blues’ team news, Dario Essugo (thigh), Romeo Lavia (quad) and Levi Colwill (knee) remain out. Mykhailo Mudryk is suspended, as he has been for the last 12 months.
Jorell Hato , who picked up an injury in the half-time warm-up on Saturday, is also not expected to recover.
Like Hato, Wesley Fofana was also only a substitute in Gameweek 18. There was no injury behind that decision, just ongoing minute management.
Managed game-time is also something that Reece James and Cole Palmer have had at points in 2025/26, although James has twice started three games in a week over the last month or so.
Managed game-time is also something that Reece James and Cole Palmer have had at points in 2025/26, although James has twice started three games in a week over the last month or so.

Amid reports that Antoine Semenyo is keen to secure a move away as quickly as possible in the January transfer window, Iraola reiterated his desire to keep the winger for as long as possible, rather than bank the transfer fee early.
Injury-wise, Tyler Adams (knee), Veljko Milosavljevic (knee) and Ben Gannon-Doak (hamstring) remain on the sidelines.
Injury-wise, Tyler Adams (knee), Veljko Milosavljevic (knee) and Ben Gannon-Doak (hamstring) remain on the sidelines.
Injury-wise, Tyler Adams (knee), Veljko Milosavljevic (knee) and Ben Gannon-Doak (hamstring) remain on the sidelines.
Alex Scott will also have to be assessed after he took a knock to the jaw at Brentford.
Matty Cash and Boubacar Kamara will both miss out after picking up their fifth yellow cards of the Premier League season against Chelsea.
Matty Cash and Boubacar Kamara will both miss out after picking up their fifth yellow cards of the Premier League season against Chelsea.
Injury-wise, Ross Barkley (knee) will remain out. Evann Guessand is away to the Africa Cup of Nations, too.
Injury-wise, Ross Barkley (knee) will remain out. Evann Guessand is away to the Africa Cup of Nations, too.
Pau Torres (calf) and Tyrone Mings (hamstring) should be nearing a return, having been “more or less” two weeks away before Gameweek 17.
Pau Torres (calf) and Tyrone Mings (hamstring) should be nearing a return, having been “more or less” two weeks away before Gameweek 17.
Harvey Elliott has officially been ill recently – but the surplus-to-demands loanee would not have featured anyway.
Fabian Hurzeler was up bright and early on Monday morning, and he started his presser by passing Kaoru Mitoma (illness) fit.
As for Danny Welbeck , back issues have limited his involvement of late. He could start against West Ham United, however.
Adam Webster (knee) and Stefanos Tzimas (knee) remain unavailable, while Carlos Baleba is on international duty with Cameroon.
Adam Webster (knee) and Stefanos Tzimas (knee) remain unavailable, while Carlos Baleba is on international duty with Cameroon.
Adam Webster (knee) and Stefanos Tzimas (knee) remain unavailable, while Carlos Baleba is on international duty with Cameroon.
The end is in sight with Solly March ‘s (knee) recovery, at least.
Yankuba Minteh was benched against Arsenal, with Hurzeler perhaps suggesting after full-time that his omission was not just tactical.
Everton have so far been cagey on providing a timeline for Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall (hamstring).
Moyes said ahead of Gameweek 18 that Jarrad Branthwaite (hamstring) was “ not due to integrate back in for at least another two to three weeks, maybe more .”
Seamus Coleman (hamstring) has been out since late November.
As for Jack Grealish , he only missed out against Burnley due to illness . Moyes said he was hopeful “we’ll get him back this week sometime”.
There are two Toffees we won’t see for sure: Iliman Ndiaye and Idrissa Gana Gueye are at the Africa Cup of Nations.
There are two Toffees we won’t see for sure: Iliman Ndiaye and Idrissa Gana Gueye are at the Africa Cup of Nations.
After the Manchester City game, however, Dyche had confirmed that Chris Wood (knee) will be out for “weeks” more after undergoing minor surgery.
Elsewhere, Ola Aina (hamstring) is nearing a return from injury and played for the B team last week . He’s not in the 25-man Premier squad, however, so can’t feature for now.
Ryan Yates (hamstring) is expected to remain out until January. He was running on the grass around a week ago but not yet in team training.
Dan Ndoye has missed the last two games but we haven’t got a timeline on him.
The Tricky Trees are also down two players due to Africa Cup of Nations involvement: Willy Boly and Ibrahim Sangare are away with the Ivory Coast.
The Tricky Trees are also down two players due to Africa Cup of Nations involvement: Willy Boly and Ibrahim Sangare are away with the Ivory Coast.

The Burnley boss at least provided an update on Maxime Esteve , who has missed the last two league games. Unless Parker misspoke, it sounds more like the centre-back is being targeted for a Gameweek 20 return.
After the draw with Everton, Parker said that it “doesn’t look good” on the knee injury that Josh Cullen sustained that same day.
Joe Worrall missed out on the stalemate at Turf Moor with a “niggle”.
Jordan Beyer (hamstring), Connor Roberts (Achilles) and Zeki Amdouni (knee) are the long-term absentees, while Lyle Foster, Hannibal Mejbri and Axel Tuanzebe are at the Africa Cup of Nations.
Jordan Beyer (hamstring), Connor Roberts (Achilles) and Zeki Amdouni (knee) are the long-term absentees, while Lyle Foster, Hannibal Mejbri and Axel Tuanzebe are at the Africa Cup of Nations.

The only question vaguely relevant to team news fans concerned Nuno’s decision not to start Callum Wilson of late.
We’re not aware of any fitness concerns, although Aaron Wan-Bissaka and El Hadji Malick Diouf are still at the Africa Cup of Nations.
We’re not aware of any fitness concerns, although Aaron Wan-Bissaka and El Hadji Malick Diouf are still at the Africa Cup of Nations.
Loanee Igor Julio also can’t face his parent club.
Rob Edwards said that Yerson Mosquera was hopefully only suffering from cramp in the defeat to Liverpool, while Santiago Bueno sustained a knock.
Rob Edwards said that Yerson Mosquera was hopefully only suffering from cramp in the defeat to Liverpool, while Santiago Bueno sustained a knock.
Andre will miss out against Manchester United after being booked for the fifth time this season.

Toti Gomes (hamstring), Jean-Ricner Bellegarde (hamstring), Marshall Munetsi (calf), Rodrigo Gomes (groin) and Leon Chiwome (knee) also remain sidelined, while Emmanuel Agbadou and Tawanda Chirewa are at the Africa Cup of Nations.
